description: Best practices and optimization patterns for AMDGPU kernels, derived from aiter/CK and AMD optimization guides

patterns.name: Double Buffering / Ping-Pong LDS
patterns.description: Use two LDS buffers alternately: while computing on buffer A, prefetch next data into buffer B. This overlaps memory latency with computation, maximizing pipeline utilization.
patterns.performance_impact: Can improve GEMM performance by 30-60% by hiding memory latency.
patterns.implementation_notes: Allocate 2x LDS space. Use s_waitcnt lgkmcnt carefully to synchronize buffer swaps. The CK comp_v4 pipeline uses DoubleSmemBuffer=true.

patterns.name: Software Pipelining (Async Load + Compute Overlap)
patterns.description: Structure the main loop so that loads for iteration N+1 are issued before computing iteration N. The hardware can overlap outstanding memory requests with ALU execution.
patterns.performance_impact: Reduces effective memory latency from ~300 cycles to near-zero for compute-bound kernels.
patterns.implementation_notes: Issue loads early in the loop iteration. Use partial s_waitcnt (vmcnt(N) where N>0) to wait only for the loads you need now.

patterns.name: MFMA Accumulator Chaining
patterns.description: Chain multiple MFMA instructions that write to the same accumulator registers. The hardware can pipeline consecutive MFMAs, achieving near-peak throughput.
patterns.performance_impact: Sustained MFMA throughput approaches theoretical peak when properly chained.
patterns.reference: AMD CDNA3 ISA manual; aiter CK block_gemm patterns
patterns.implementation_notes: Issue MFMA instructions back-to-back on the same accumulator registers. Interleave MFMA with data movement to hide AccVGPR read latency.

patterns.name: Vectorized Global Memory Access (128-bit)
patterns.description: Use 128-bit (dwordx4) global loads and stores to maximize memory bandwidth utilization. Each 128-bit load moves 4x more data per instruction.
applicable_to: All kernels with contiguous memory access
patterns.performance_impact: Up to 4x reduction in memory instruction count. Better utilization of cache lines.
patterns.implementation_notes: Use float4/half8 types. Ensure addresses are 16-byte aligned. The compiler auto-vectorizes with -O3 in many cases.

patterns.name: LDS Padding for Bank Conflict Avoidance
patterns.description: Add +1 padding to shared memory array dimensions to avoid bank conflicts. LDS has 32 banks; stride-32 access causes all threads to collide.
patterns.performance_impact: Eliminates LDS bank conflicts, can improve LDS-bound kernels by 10-32x.
patterns.reference: AMD LDS banking documentation; aiter CK shared memory layouts
patterns.implementation_notes: Change float smem[N][M] to float smem[N][M+1] (or M+PADDING). The padding offset distributes bank accesses.

patterns.name: Occupancy-Aware Register Usage
patterns.description: Balance register usage against occupancy. On CDNA3, each SIMD has 512 VGPRs. Using <=64 VGPRs allows 8 waves/SIMD (max occupancy).
patterns.performance_impact: Higher occupancy helps hide memory latency for memory-bound kernels. Compute-bound kernels may benefit from more registers.
patterns.reference: AMD Occupancy Calculator; rocprof-compute occupancy analysis
patterns.implementation_notes: Use __launch_bounds__(block_size, min_blocks) to hint the compiler. Trade off between register spilling and occupancy.

patterns.name: Stream-K Scheduling for Load Balancing
patterns.description: Instead of assigning fixed tiles to blocks, use stream-K scheduling where blocks dynamically pick up work from a global counter. This ensures all CUs stay busy even when work is unevenly distributed.
patterns.performance_impact: 10-30% improvement for non-square or irregular-shaped GEMMs.
patterns.reference: aiter CK gemm_streamk/; CUTLASS Stream-K paper
patterns.implementation_notes: Each block atomically increments a global tile counter, processes the assigned tile, then loops for the next. Requires careful handling of partial tiles at boundaries.

patterns.name: Warp-Specialized Pipeline (Producer-Consumer)
patterns.description: Assign different roles to different warps within a block: some warps load data (producers), others compute (consumers). This enables persistent pipelining.
applicable_to: Large kernels with complex data movement
patterns.performance_impact: Can improve utilization on complex pipelines with heterogeneous operations.
patterns.reference: CUTLASS warp-specialized GEMM; aiter advanced pipeline patterns
patterns.implementation_notes: Requires careful synchronization between producer and consumer warps via LDS barriers or flags.
